prerequisites: An active Copper account with permission to access System Settings (required to generate an API key).
An API key generated from Copper System Settings (maps to the required secret config field api_key).
The email address of the Copper user who generated the API key (maps to the required config field user_email; the API requires the token owner's email on every request).

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Copper authenticates each REST request with an API key passed in the X-PW-AccessToken header. The connector's single secret field api_key holds this key. Every request must also send X-PW-Application set to developer_api, X-PW-UserEmail set to the email of the user who generated the token (the connector's user_email field), and Content-Type set to application/json.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Copper API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Copper and open System Settings, then go to API Keys and click GENERATE API KEY. You can label the key for organizational purposes. Copy the generated key into the connector's api_key field. Note that Copper supports multiple valid API keys at once.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token owner's email
setupSteps.body: Enter the email address of the Copper user who generated the API key into the user_email field. Copper requires this email on every API request via the X-PW-UserEmail header; it must match the user who created the token.

setupSteps.title: Verify the connection
setupSteps.body: With api_key and user_email set, the connector authenticates by sending X-PW-AccessToken (your API key), X-PW-Application: developer_api, X-PW-UserEmail (your email), and Content-Type: application/json. Be aware Copper limits API calls to 180 requests per minute and returns HTTP 429 when the limit is exceeded.

sources.title: Copper API requests (required headers and rate limits)
